name: The Long Crossing
objective: Cross the Overgrowth west to east — raise the three relay pylons to wake the dormant Anchor, then reach the east gate.
title: Chapter I — The Long Crossing

intro.title: After the Anchorfall
lines: The night the Anchors fell, the frontier forgot itself.
Roads unwrote. Names ran like wet ink.
The Anchorcraft carries the last operators east.

intro.title: The Long Crossing
lines: Beyond the meadow, the Overgrowth has swallowed the old road.
Three dormant relay pylons sleep in its clearings.
Raise them all, and the east gate remembers how to open.

outro.title: First Light
lines: The first Anchor settles. Finality, even out here.
The crew rests by the fire. One road remembered.
Eastward, the basin glows wrong.

npcs.name: Doc Aroyo
lines: Hold still — there. Good as before the Fall. Almost.
This camp held three nightwaves. The garrison didn’t. I stayed for the patients.
Hm, hm-hmm... sorry. Old transit jingle. The lines will run again once the Anchor settles.
A barricade and a working turret would do more for my patients than I ever could.
Watch the Lurkers in the green. They wait buried until you’re close, then they aren’t buried.

npcs.name: Wrenna Halv
lines: Operator on your feet — good. The Anchor east of here is dormant. Cold since the Fall.
Three relay pylons feed it. All three online or the gate stays cold — quorums don’t negotiate.
Pylons take LYTH. The Entropy drops shards when it falls — kill it, take them, build.
Crystal nodes grow along the road. Crack them open; unsealed LYTH helps nobody but the Leeches.
People lie. Quorums don’t. Get my three pylons up and we all go home.

npcs.name: Cassio ‘Quorum’ Bell
lines: Three pylons for one gate. Counted twice on my fingers. I don’t like the odds.
I don’t run jobs under seven-in-ten. This lane? Coin flip. That’s why I’m sitting down.
Nightwave took the last courier through here. Keep to the clearings, operator.
Forklings in the trees. See one, there are already two. That’s how forks work.

npcs.name: Old Tarn
lines: Stranded two seasons in this green. Thought nobody kept the signal.
Here — shards I pulled before my lantern drowned. The LYTH remembers who carried it. Fondly, in my case.
Wet leaves hide dry teeth. Walk the open ground, not the thicket.
A Phantom past the ridge wears an operator’s coat. It is not an operator. Burn anything classical it offers.
Settle the Anchor. Once it settles, that’s finality — even out here.
